title: leucanthemum monspeliense
intro: Leucanthemum monspeliense, also known as French tansy or pyrethrum, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like blooms and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to repel insects and parasites due to its pyrethrum content, a natural insecticide. The health benefits of Leucanthemum monspeliense are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in soothing skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be used in various forms, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
